title: Stock Market Returns by Year: S&P 500 Annual Performance ...
description: Discover the complete, shocking history of the stock market. We compiled 97 years of S&P 500 annual returns — see the highest highs, lowest lows, and exactly what happened last year.

schemas.headline: Stock Market Returns by Year: S&P 500 Annual Performance 1928–2026
schemas.description: Complete table of S&P 500 annual returns from 1928 to 2026. Includes interactive chart, average returns, and historical performance statistics.

mainEntity.name: What are the stock market returns by year?

acceptedAnswer.text: The stock market, as measured by the S&P 500, has generated positive returns in roughly 73% of years since 1928. Returns vary widely by year, ranging from gains of over 50% to losses of over 40%.

mainEntity.name: What are the S&P 500 annual returns on average?

acceptedAnswer.text: The historical average annual return for the S&P 500 from 1928 to 2024 is approximately 11.9%. However, the median return is higher, at around 15.2%, because a few very bad years drag the arithmetic average down.

mainEntity.name: Where can I find the S&P 500 yearly performance history?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our complete S&P 500 yearly performance history table provides the total return (including dividends) for every year from 1928 to the present day, allowing you to track historical trends across nearly a century of market data.

mainEntity.name: What is the average stock market return per year?

acceptedAnswer.text: While short-term returns are highly volatile, the long-term average stock market return per year is historically around 10-12% before inflation, assuming dividends are reinvested.

mainEntity.name: How many years does the stock market go down?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, the stock market goes down about 1 out of every 4 years. From 1928 to 2024, the S&P 500 recorded negative annual returns in 26 out of 97 years.
